title: descurainia sagittata
intro: Descurainia sagittata, also known as tansy mustard or western tansy mustard, is a biennial or perennial plant in the mustard family that grows up to 3-4 feet tall, producing yellow flowers in clusters. The plant's roots, leaves, and stems have been used in herbal medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Descurainia sagittata include reducing inflammation and pain, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also sometimes used in herbal capsules or supplements.
